title: Sapiensly App Manifest v1 (MVP subset)
description: MVP subset of the App manifest schema. Excludes workflows, connections, code-custom field types (formula/lookup/rollup), media field types (file/image/rich_text/percentage/time), and write/interactive blocks (form/button/modal/chart/kanban/calendar/list/tabs/accordion). These layers will be added in later phases without breaking changes.

id.description: Prefixed identifier: a short lowercase tag (2-5 chars) + underscore + 8-60 alphanumeric/underscore chars. A standard ULID (26 chars) fits comfortably. Examples: app_01j..., obj_01j..., fld_01j... Wider than strict ULID to forgive the AI generating slightly off-spec ids; the runtime treats the value as opaque after the validator accepts it. The sys_ namespace is reserved for system virtual fields (sys_created_at, sys_updated_at).

full_bleed.description: Make the section span the full content width (edge to edge) instead of sitting inside the page padding. Use on top-level coloured sections.

gradient.description: Linear-gradient background (overrides `background`). Keep `from`/`to` in the same tonal range so the auto-contrast text colour reads against both ends.

source_definition.description: Backing store for an object. Absent or {type:internal} = the records store (default). {type:connected} = an external system reached through an integration (connected object).

integration_id.description: The authorized integration this object reads/writes through.

id_path.description: Dot path to the external record id within a row.

operations.description: Maps each CRUD operation to an external request. Omit create/update for a read-only connected object.

field_map.description: Ties manifest fields to external response paths. Partial-tolerant: unmapped fields render null.

path.description: Path appended to the integration base URL; may template {id}.

collection_path.description: Dot path to the row array in a list response (e.g. "results").

page_param.description: Query param name for pagination, when supported.

page_mode.description: How page_param is expressed: a raw offset (default) or a 1-based page number derived from offset/limit.

page_size_param.description: Query param name for the page size (the query's limit), when supported.

sort_param.description: Query param name to sort by; its value is the sort field's external_path.

order_param.description: Optional query param name for the sort direction (asc/desc).

filter_params.description: Pushes simple equality filters down to external query params. Non-equality conditions degrade gracefully (not pushed down).

max_length.description: Maximum length of the plain-text content (HTML tags excluded).

mime_types.description: Optional MIME allowlist. Accepts wildcards like image/*. When omitted, any MIME is allowed (subject to the global size limit).

field_condition.description: A single structured condition over another field of the SAME form, evaluated reactively against the live form values. Used by visible_if / required_if. `value` is a literal compared against the referenced field's current value (an array for in/not_in; omitted for is_null/is_not_null/is_truthy/is_falsy).

size.description: Visual size override, independent of the semantic `level`. Use `xl`/`2xl`/`display` for website section titles; defaults follow `level`.

oneOf.description: Action column: renders a button per row. on_click resolves {{row.id}} and {{row.data.<slug>}} from the current row at click time.

icon.description: Optional emoji shown in the card corner.

compare.description: Optional comparison query (e.g. the previous period). When set the card shows the % change vs this value as a trend chip.

delta_good.description: Which direction of change is GOOD (green): 'up' (more is better) or 'down' (less is better, e.g. costs/churn).

variant.description: Tone of the card — drives its colour and default icon.

icon.description: Optional emoji override; otherwise a default per variant is used.

record_id_expression.description: Required when mode=edit. Expression resolving to the record id, e.g. {{params.id}}.

description.description: Optional sub-title shown below the title; also drives aria-describedby.

icon.description: An emoji (🌍) or a Lucide icon name.

field_id.description: Field whose values become words; each distinct value is sized by how often it occurs.

left_fraction.description: 1-11; right takes 12 - left_fraction. Default 4 = 1/3 left, 2/3 right.

oneOf.description: Open a modal block by id. Optional `params` are injected as the modal's local context — read inside the modal's child blocks with {{params.<key>}}, including the form's record_id_expression for an Edit modal that needs the row id.

params.description: Optional key→value map. Values can be expression strings ({{row.id}}, {{form.x}}) or literal scalars. Each key becomes available inside the modal as {{params.<key>}}.

values.description: Field-slug → value map. Values can be expression strings ({{form.x}}, {{row.id}}, {{current_user.id}}) OR literal scalars (true, 42, "open", null). RecordWriteService casts them to the field's type on save.

Field-slug → value map. Values can be expression strings ({{form.x}}, {{row.id}}, {{current_user.id}}) OR literal scalars (true, 42, "open", null). RecordWriteService casts them to the field's type on save.

value.description: Literal or expression — resolved before assignment.

items.description: Expression resolving to an array, e.g. {{steps.calc.output.primes}}. The sub-steps run once per element.

item_variable.description: Variable name the current element is bound to (default 'item'), referenced via {{vars.<name>}}.

index_variable.description: Optional variable bound to the 0-based index.

code.description: JavaScript run in an isolated QuickJS sandbox (no network/filesystem/host access). Receives the resolved `input` map as the `input` argument; whatever it `return`s becomes the step output.

input.description: Map of name → expression resolved against the workflow context before the script runs.

theme.description: Color palette applied to the runtime: 'light' (default, clean white page) or 'dark' (dark surfaces).

accent.description: Brand accent colour (hex). Drives buttons, links and highlights across the app so everything looks cohesive. Set one per site.

font.description: Body/heading font family family: sans (modern), serif (editorial), rounded (friendly), mono (technical).

brand.description: Site identity shown in the header.
